evals.prompt: How do I clean my email list with SafetyMails?
evals.expected_output: Guidance on bulk verification upload process including file upload, 19-step algorithm, and result interpretation

assertions.name: mentions_bulk_upload
assertions.description: Mentions uploading a list (CSV, TXT) to SafetyMails panel for bulk verification

assertions.name: mentions_verification_algorithm
assertions.description: Mentions the 19-step verification algorithm or describes the verification checks

assertions.name: mentions_credit_cost
assertions.description: Mentions that bulk verification costs 1 credit per email

evals.prompt: I want to add real-time email verification to my signup form using SafetyMails
evals.expected_output: Guidance on setting up the SafetyMails real-time API JavaScript embed for form validation

assertions.name: mentions_javascript_embed
assertions.description: Mentions the real-time API is a JavaScript embed or snippet for forms

assertions.name: mentions_api_key
assertions.description: Mentions generating an API key from the SafetyMails panel

assertions.name: mentions_response_time
assertions.description: Mentions sub-1-second response time or real-time validation speed

evals.prompt: How does SafetyMails Email Finder work and what does it cost?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of Email Finder feature with input requirements and credit economics

assertions.name: mentions_inputs
assertions.description: Mentions that Email Finder requires first name, last name, and company domain as inputs

assertions.name: mentions_credit_cost
assertions.description: Mentions 7 credits per found email (and 0 credits if not found)

assertions.name: mentions_auto_verification
assertions.description: Mentions that found emails are automatically verified
